titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Richard Merkin Collection
titlestmt.author: by Beinecke staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Saturday December 30, 2017 at 05:06

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.physdesc: (57 boxes) + 4 broadsides, 1 roll, and 1 record album storage

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition note
acqinfo.p: June 2010 Acquisitions: purchased from Derringer Books on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, the Alfred Z. Baker, Jr. Fund, and the Danford N. Barney, Jr. Fund.
December 2010 Acquisition: purchased from Appledore Books on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund.
September 2011 Acquisition: purchased from Jeff Hirsch Books on the Donald Windham-Sandy Campbell Collection Endowment Fund.

accessrestrict.head: Information about Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.
Boxes 40, 60, 61 (audiovisual materials): Restricted fragile material. For further information consult the appropriate curator.

userestrict.head: Ownership Copyright
userestrict.p: The Richard Merkin Collection is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

prefercite.head: Cite As
prefercite.p: Richard Merkin Collection. General Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

processinfo.head: Processing Notes
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections as they are acquired, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.

This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization. Various acquisitions associated with the collection have not been merged and organized as a whole. Each acquisition is described separately in the contents list below and titled according to month and year of acquisition.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

bioghist.head: Richard Merkin (1938-2009)
bioghist.p: Richard Merkin was an American painter, illustrtor, and arts educator.

scopecontent.head: Description of the Papers
scopecontent.p: [scope and content]

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into three groupings: June 2010 Acquisitions; December 2010 Acquisition; and September 2011 Acquisition.

did.unittitle: New York Correspondence School correspondence and papers

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Contains correspondence, artwork, photographs, printed material and ephemera by, to or relating to Ray Johnson, New York Correspondence School and Richard Merkin.

did.unittitle: Correspondence - Merkin, Richard - New York Correspondence School
Correspondence - Merkin, Richard - New York Correspondence School

Correspondence - Merkin, Richard - New York Correspondence School
Correspondence - Merkin, Richard - New York Correspondence School
Printed material - New York Correspondence School
Richard Merkin and Virginia F. Fritz

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Contains correspondence, photographs, artwork, printed material and ephemera by, to, or relating to Virginia F. Fritz and Richard Merkin.

did.unittitle: Merkin, Richard - Drawings on napkins and photographs
Fritz, Virginia F. - The always book

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Wishbones discarded. Preservation photocopy made.

did.unittitle: Shoebox lids - Virginia Fritz to Richard Merkin

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access note
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. For further information consult the appropriate curator.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Collage contains a photograph of Richard Merkin.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Contains a photograph of Richard Merkin (?) as a boy in Tarzan costume, with a homemade matte.

did.unittitle: Framed artwork - "I love you, bunny, Easter '68"

did.physdesc: 29.43 linear feet (49 boxes) + 4 broadsides, 1 roll, and 1 record album storage

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Contains Mail Art, Personal material, Correspondence, Erotic material, Popular Culture material, Scrapbooks, Albums, Journals, Sports material, Printed material, and Audiovisual material.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Mail or Correspondence Art sent to Richard Merkin. Includes typical correspondence as well.

did.unittitle: Mail art removed from correspondence files

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Includes Jann Haworth correspondence, and contains letters which reference Lucian Freud, John Lennon, and R.B. Kitaj.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Includes Seaver Leslie correspondence.

did.unittitle: Mail art and offbeat letters and announcements"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Correspondent may also use the pseudonym "Darbus-Warbus".

did.unittitle: The New Yorker - Vanity Fair - GQ

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Correspondent also goes by "Mary", and may also use the pseudonym "Pumpkin".

did.unittitle: Rhode Island School of Design - presidents

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Includes notebooks, photographs, printed material, clippings, artwork, and various manuscript materials.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Contains two baby books.

did.unittitle: Erasmus Hall HS - yearbook and diploma

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Includes eight McX covers, by Todd McEwen, circa 1990.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Contains four copies.

did.unittitle: Rhode Island School of Design diplomas

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Also includes family photographs and artwork.

did.unittitle: Misc. photographs of work, clippings, letters, etc.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Contains three copies.

did.unittitle: Contact prints and transparencies of various subjects and artwork

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Includes photographs of Dorothy Dalton, Fannie Brice and the movie The Blue Angel (1930).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Portraits are made out to "Madeline Fuller", who is also nicknamed "Sunny" or "The Sunshine Girl".

did.unittitle: "Sketches of NYU students and faculty", sketchbook
Caricatures of Rita Hayworth and Katherine Hepburn

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Contains three booklets of various caricatures.
Bookelt #1 includes: Mickey Rooney, Clark Gable, Boris Karloff, Chico Marx, Harold Lloyd, Ben Turpin, Stan Laurel and Oliver Hardy, Buster Keaton, Groucho Marx, and Harpo Marx
Booklet #2 includes: Wallace Berry, Bela Lugosi, Peter Lorre, Bob Hope, Charles Laughton, Greta Garbo, Fernandel, Maurice Chevalier, and William Powell.
Booklet #3 includes: Gary Cooper, Jimmy Durante, Marilyn Monroe, Marlene Dietrich, Edward G. Robinson, and Fred Astaire.

did.unittitle: "Driver - to the [Luxor]" - Pen sketches on cardboard for Richard Merkin
Printed collage, from book or proof

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: "Eastman" is written on back. Photograph may be taken in Cuba.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Painting shows a representation of Mary Louise "Texas" Guinan.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: No records are present.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Albums appear to be from different families. Notable images include photographs of silent-movie actress Jacqueline Logan and bandleader Mal Hallett.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Contains correspondence, clippings, albums, typescripts, photographs, and art work.

scopecontent.p: Photograph includes various players, including Dizzie Dean, Paul Dean, Leo Durocher (?), and Frankie Frisch (?). Teams represented include the House of David, the St. Louis Cardinals, the Boston Braves, the High School Athletic Association, and others.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Includes exhibition catalogs, exhibition fliers, exhibition posters, art magazines, and various printed material.

scopecontent.p: Contains sticky notes on various pages, as well as business card, "With the Compliments of Edith Gregor Halpert". Edith Gregor Halpert was the director of The Downtown Gallery.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents note
scopecontent.p: Contains items removed from various printed material.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access note
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. For further information consult the appropriate curator.
